svg.text: Lyapunov sign points to a regime. Orbit density tells you how that regime is occupied.
Same logistic forcing idea, but now with tail histograms and last-iterate traces. Near-zero cases spread into many bands before they look fully chaotic.

The point of the sidecar is not that λ becomes useless. It is that sign alone does not tell you whether the orbit collapses into two spikes, spreads across a few thin bands, or fills most of the interval.
